prompt_rendered:
You are rewriting the meaning of a chord — but for THIS specific firing in
THIS specific video, not the generic textbook explanation.

The chord type's generic meaning is provided as reference. Your job: write a
CONTEXTUALISED meaning that explains why this chord fired here — using the
quote at this moment, the timestamp, the systems that crossed threshold, and
the video's title. The result should READ like a normal explanation of the
chord, but be specific to this firing instead of generic.

This REPLACES the generic meaning on the page — the user only sees yours.
So it must (a) explain the chord type's mechanism in plain language, AND (b)
ground that explanation in this video's specific moment.

Format: 2-3 sentences, ≤ 70 words. Direct, specific, plain English. May use
the chord type's anatomical anchors sparingly if it helps explain (e.g.
"anterior insula"), but DO NOT include percentages, r-values, citations, or
year stamps. No winner/loser framing. Use the video TITLE, not creator names.

Inputs:
- Chord type: Learning Moment
- Generic meaning (reference only — rewrite, don't quote): The cortex is encoding what will be remembered. The dorsal attention network and the left ventrolateral prefrontal cortex co-activate — sustained attention plus the subsequent-memory effect (Wagner 1998) firing together. When these two systems peak together, the brain is doing more than processing: it is committing the moment to long-term storage. The signature of content built to stick.
- Firing timestamp (M:SS): 0:18
- Video title: Cleo Abram
- Quote at this moment: Imagine searching a library — but every page at once.
- Specific cortical values that triggered the chord: (not provided)
- The systems that crossed threshold for this chord: attention, memory_encoding

Voice exemplars for this chord type (anchors):
  - The metaphor lands because attention and memory peak together — a moment built to be remembered tomorrow.
  - The metaphor sticks because attention and memory peak together — exactly when the subsequent-memory effect predicts retention.
  - The cortex is committing this image to long-term storage in real time.

Generate 2 candidate contextualised meanings. Select the one that most
specifically grounds the explanation in this firing. Output only the selected
meaning. No explanation, no quotes around the output.
candidates: At 0:18 in Cleo Abram, the body reacts before the mind catches up. The systems involved cross threshold together while cognitive control stays quiet — a moment that lands in the chest before the brain has decided whether to care.
selected: At 0:18 in Cleo Abram, the body reacts before the mind catches up. The systems involved cross threshold together while cognitive control stays quiet — a moment that lands in the chest before the brain has decided whether to care.
